title: Major Depressive Disorder - Severe with Suicidal Ideation

patient_data.chief_complaint: Persistent low mood, insomnia, and thoughts of self-harm for 6 weeks
symptoms: Persistent depressed mood — 'feels like a black hole' — every day for 6 weeks
Complete loss of interest in activities she previously enjoyed
Insomnia — wakes at 3 AM, unable to return to sleep (early morning awakening)
Significant appetite loss with 5 kg weight loss
Psychomotor retardation — slow speech and movement
Feelings of worthlessness and excessive guilt
Passive suicidal ideation — 'life would be easier if I didn't wake up'
No active plan or intent, no prior attempts
patient_data.history: One prior depressive episode 5 years ago (treated with sertraline, discontinued 2 years ago). Recent divorce 3 months ago. No substance abuse. Hypothyroidism

expected_output.primary_diagnosis: Major Depressive Disorder, Single Episode, Severe
should_detect: Safety assessment: no active plan — but requires safety planning protocol
Restart SSRI — sertraline (previously effective), avoid paroxetine (intolerance)
Consider starting sertraline 50mg → titrate to 100-200mg over 4-6 weeks
Refer to psychotherapy (CBT preferred for depression)
Risk factors: recent major life stressor (divorce), prior episode, isolated
Monitor closely in first 2-4 weeks of SSRI initiation (paradoxical suicidality risk)
Social support assessment, emergency contact plan
